reason_for_decision: The article passes as a high-level compliance-planning piece because it stays within non-legal-advice boundaries while still giving project teams concrete process guidance.
strengths: The article clearly defines itself as operational guidance rather than legal advice, which keeps the framing disciplined.
It is strong on end-use clarity, document consistency, and engineering-compliance synchronization.
The 2024 regulatory updates are integrated in a way that helps program teams understand why the workflow changed.
summary_assessment: This article passes comfortably. It is appropriately cautious, practically useful, and well aligned with the needs of project managers handling dual-use export workflows.
